title: Gospel of Truth
goal: Accuracy-first translation prep that preserves poetic theology and Valentinian terminology.

cross_reference_strategy.notes: Use XII.2 overlap to illuminate damaged or uncertain places in I.3.

guardrails: Preserve reified Valentinian terms where flattening them would erase the theology.
When XII.2 materially clarifies I.3, mark that repair explicitly instead of silently blending the witnesses.
Do not naturalize the text into generic devotional English.

witnesses.notes: Primary Coptic facsimile witness from Nag Hammadi Codex I, tractate 3. Curated page list (16-23, 25-30, 32-41, 43) sourced via the Gospels.net manuscript page and fetched from Claremont CCDL image records. OCR completed 2026-04-22 via Vertex-backed Gemini 3.1 Pro using a fixed crop box (0.16,0.03,0.84,0.97) and 6-band page assembly. Interior pages are clean; page 16 and page 43 remain the natural boundary edge-cases.

instructions: Transcribe Coptic exactly before any normalization.
Preserve line breaks and page boundaries when visible.
Mark uncertain or damaged letters explicitly instead of guessing.
Do not silently harmonize spelling or punctuation.
Emit plaintext plus a short uncertainty note if the witness is hard to read.
ocr_job.notes: Primary Coptic facsimile witness from Nag Hammadi Codex I, tractate 3. Curated page list (16-23, 25-30, 32-41, 43) sourced via the Gospels.net manuscript page and fetched from Claremont CCDL image records. Completed against the staged Codex I.3 facsimile set with Vertex-backed Gemini 3.1 Pro. 23/25 pages finished with all-region STOP; the boundary pages 16 and 43 each retained one MAX_TOKENS region.

witnesses.notes: English translation / interpretation witness only; not a Coptic-script primary source. Primary online Coptic/translation witness for the complete I.3 copy.

meta.title: Gospel of Truth — Gospels.net
meta.license_excerpt: The following translation has been committed to the public domain and may be freely copied and used, changed or unchanged, for any purpose. It is based on the Coptic text of Nag Hammadi Codex I, 3. For information about the surviving manuscripts of the Gospel of Truth, see the Manuscript Information page. For additional information about the translation, see the introduction to the PDF version .

witnesses.notes: Fragmentary XII.2 overlap witness for damaged or uncertain stretches of I.3. OCR completed 2026-04-22 with 100% Coptic-letter coverage on every fragment. Per-fragment segment mapping + text paths in the OCR job file.
witnesses.coverage_label: Approximate overlap: Book of the Living (seg 004), Return to Unity (seg 005), Parable of the Jars (seg 006), Coming into Being (seg 007)

ocr_job.engine_note: Ran under the 3-pro-preview default that was in tools/coptic/run_coptic_ocr.py at the time. Outputs are clean Coptic (100% Coptic-letter coverage on every fragment). The default model has since been corrected to gemini-3.1-pro-preview; rerun under 3.1-pro is not urgent and can happen opportunistically.
instructions: Transcribe Coptic exactly before any normalization.
Preserve line breaks and page boundaries when visible.
Mark uncertain or damaged letters explicitly instead of guessing.
Do not silently harmonize spelling or punctuation.
Emit plaintext plus a short uncertainty note if the witness is hard to read.
ocr_job.notes: Fragmentary XII.2 overlap witness for damaged or uncertain stretches of I.3.

fragments.approximate_i3_alignment: NHC I.3 pp. 30-31 area — Book of the Living / early Return to Unity
fragments.alignment_basis: Attridge NHS 22-23 scholarly consensus on XII.2 extents (Zone 2 fact-level citation). Exact per-line alignment to I.3 refined during drafting.

fragments.approximate_i3_alignment: NHC I.3 pp. 31-32 area — Return to Unity
NHC I.3 pp. 34-36 area — Return to Unity / Parable of the Jars
NHC I.3 pp. 35-37 area — Parable of the Jars / Coming into Being
Multi-location fragment bucket; per-line assignment to I.3 segments deferred to drafting.
fragments.alignment_basis: Requires per-line scholarly alignment against the full NHC XII plate sequence during the drafting phase.

ocr_job.drafting_use: At drafting time, for any Gospel of Truth segment listed under coverage_segments above, the translator prompt should include the corresponding fragment's Coptic text as an overlay/cross-check witness to NHC I.3. Where XII.2 and I.3 disagree, the disagreement gets an explicit per-verse footnote rather than silent harmonization.

consult_sources.label: Attridge NHS 22-23
consult_sources.notes: Primary consultation layer for Gospel of Truth and XII.2 overlap handling.

consult_sources.label: Thomassen
consult_sources.notes: Consult on Valentinian theology and terminology in Gospel of Truth.

consult_sources.label: Meyer 2007
consult_sources.notes: Readable consultation layer, especially for Thomas and Truth.
